revision.text:
[[ja:梅木平古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Baikihira Kofun |image = [[File:Baikihira Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber |alternate_name = Baikihira Tomb No. 1{{Sfn|Place names in Hiroshima Prefecture|1982|qq=}} |group = |location = {{ill|Mihara, Hiroshima|en|Mihara, Hiroshima|qid=Q820760|lt=Mihara, Hiroshima|1=Mihara, Hiroshima}}, Hongo-cho, Shimokitakata (Baiki), {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|qid=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.4083|N|132.966056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|iso = JP-34 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Unknown |burial_facilities = Double-corridor {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Unknown |associated_tombs = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} |Interred_individual = |mausoleum = |Historic Site = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Baikihira Kofun" |notable_features = |map = Japan Hiroshima |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Baikihira Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Baikihira Kofun''' (梅木平古墳) is a {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Hongo-cho, Shimokitakata, {{ill|Mihara, Hiroshima|en|Mihara, Hiroshima|qid=Q820760|lt=Mihara, Hiroshima|1=Mihara, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|qid=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}. The tumulus is presumed to be circular in type and is Historic Site1 as a [[Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == Baikihira Kofun is situated on the low hills at the northern edge of the Nashiwa and Obara rivers, tributaries of the Numata River, in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. Currently, two Buddha statues are enshrined on the tumulus at the site known as Baikeian. The tumulus is presumed to be circular, but its exact size remains unclear{{Sfn|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|1989|qq=}}. The burial facility is a double-corridor {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. The total length of the stone chamber is 13.25 meters, making it the largest such structure in Hiroshima Prefecture. burial goods have not been identified{{Sfn|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|1989|qq=}}{{Sfn|Historic Site of Mihara City|2016|qq=}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, at the beginning of the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}{{Sfn|Historic Site of Mihara City|2016|qq=}} (alternatively, the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} to early 7th centuryInterpretive board at the site.). The site was Historic Site1 a [[Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site]] in 1949. Nearby, other tumuli such as the {{ill|Mitoshiro Kofun|en|Mitoshiro Kofun|qid=Q63386711|lt=Onendai Kofun|1=Mitoshiro Kofun}} and {{ill|Sadamaru Kofun Cluster|ja|貞丸古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadamaru Kofun Cluster|1=Sadamaru Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q61750523}}, as well as the {{ill|Yokomi temple ruins|en|Yokomi temple ruins|qid=Q11543464|lt=Yokomi Temple Ruins|1=Yokomi temple ruins}}, suggest a connection between local ruling families and the central {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingdom|1=Yamato Kingship}}. == History == * 1715 (Shōtoku 5): Mentioned in the document "Shimo-Kitakata Village Shrines and Historic Sites Register," which references Baikeian and the mound at Baikeian. * 1718 (Kyōhō 3): Document "Toyo District Shimo-Kitakata Village Report" includes a description of the mound at Baikeian{{Sfn|Place names in Hiroshima Prefecture|1982|qq=}}. * 1825 (Bunsei 8): The "Shimo-Kitakata Village map" in the {{ill|Geihan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geihan Tsuushi|1=Geihan Tsuushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11615959}} refers to the Baikeian Mound (Baikihira Tomb No. 1, this tumulus) and the Miyatsukawa Mound (Baikihira Tomb No. 2){{Sfn|Place names in Hiroshima Prefecture|1982|qq=}}. * October 28, 1949: Designated as "Baikihira Kofun (Baikeian)" by Hiroshima Prefecture. * January 26, 2012: Renamed to "Baikihira Kofun," with the historic site boundaries expanded. == burial == [[File:Baikihira Kofun, 3D-B-3.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber|qq=}}]] [[File:Baikihira Kofun, 3D-N-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed plan of the stone chamber|qq=}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-corridor {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. The chamber area are as follows{{Sfn|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|1989|qq=}}: * Total chamber length: 13.25 meters * Main chamber: Length 6.3 meters, width 3.0 meters (rear wall), height 4.2 meters (rear wall) * Corridor: Current length 6.9 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 1.9 meters The chamber lacks the original entrance, suggesting the actual length may have been slightly greater. It remains the largest stone chamber in Hiroshima Prefecture. The rear wall consists of three tiers, and the side walls are constructed of 3–4 tiers, narrowing to 1.9 meters at the ceiling{{Sfn|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|1989|qq=}}. A partition of upright stones separates the main chamber and the corridor{{Sfn|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|1989|qq=}}. The chamber ceiling consists of four large stone slabs, with a significant height difference between the ceilings of the main chamber and the corridor. Stone chambers with a double-corridor style and projecting sleeve stones, such as this one, are concentrated along the Seto Inland Sea coast in the Chūgoku and Shikoku regions and are categorized as "Kakuzuka-type chambers"Saeki Rei, "Form and Evolution of Kakuzuka-type Chambers," [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/10493 'End of the Kofun built: Political Relations in Setouchi and Surrounding Areas'], Kochi University, 2012 (link via Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site).. Baikihira Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kofun and Baikeian Baikihira Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Baikihira Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward corridor) Baikihira Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward main chamber) Baikihira Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward entrance) == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** Baikihira Kofun **: Designated on October 28, 1949, as "Baikihira Kofun (Baikeian).". **: Renamed to "Baikihira Kofun" and expanded on January 26, 2012[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120500.html Baikihira Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education).Hiroshima Prefecture Report, January 26, 2012, {{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/soumu/soumu/kenpo/pdf24/01gatsu/007/20120126180002.pdf Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education Announcement No. 2]|qq=}}.. == notes == {{Note list|qq=}} == References == * Interpretive board (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education and typeer Hongo Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Baikihira Kofun|title=Place names in Hiroshima Prefecture|series={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Dictionary|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place names Dictionary|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}}, Volume 35|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Place names in Hiroshima Prefecture|1982|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Ko Miyato|year=1989|chapter=Baikihira Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|1989|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=Baikihira Kofun|title=Historic Site of Mihara City|publisher=Mihara City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historic Site of Mihara City|2016|qq=}}|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Mitoshiro Kofun|en|Mitoshiro Kofun|qid=Q63386711|lt=Onendai Kofun|1=Mitoshiro Kofun}} * {{ill|Sadamaru Kofun Cluster|ja|貞丸古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadamaru Kofun Cluster|1=Sadamaru Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q61750523}} * {{ill|Yokomi temple ruins|en|Yokomi temple ruins|qid=Q11543464|lt=Yokomi Temple Ruins|1=Yokomi temple ruins}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Baikihira Kofun|qq=}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120500.html Baikihira Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|梅木平古墳|version=102317203|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:梅木平古墳]] {{Commons category|Baikihira Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q61750527}} [[Category:三原市の歴史]] [[Category:Round Kofun]]
